name: Volcán de la Granitilla — circular desde Níjar
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Níjar traverse a unique landscape characterized by its volcanic origins and semi-desert hinterland within the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park. The region features rugged volcanic rock formations, arid canyons, and a pristine coastline with secluded coves. This diverse terrain offers varied routes for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Níjar The most popular mountain bike trail is Cortijo del Fraile and Rodalquilar Mines Loop, a 18.9 miles (30.5 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 4 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the historic mining landscapes and offers views of the surrounding volcanic terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cañón de Rellana Artesica — circular desde Agua Amarga — Parque Natural del Cabo de Gata-Níjar, an easy 13.0 miles (21.0 km) path. This route winds through canyons and offers scenic views near Agua Amarga. Local mountain bikers also love the Minas del Oro — circular desde Fernán Pérez — Parque Natural del Cabo de Gata-Níjar, a 21.1 miles (34.0 km) trail leading through historic gold mining areas, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Níjar is defined by volcanic terrain, semi-desert landscapes, and coastal paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to challenging technical descents. The routes in Níjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Níjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Níjar?
A: Níjar offers over 100 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. The komoot community has explored 107 routes in the area, with 22 easy, 67 moderate, and 18 difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Níjar?
A: The terrain in Níjar is highly diverse due to its volcanic origins and semi-desert hinterland within the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park. You'll encounter rugged volcanic rock formations, arid canyons, and packed-earth tracks, as well as coastal paths with views of secluded coves and the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Níjar?
A: Yes, Níjar has several family-friendly options. For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030977349/canon-de-rellana-artesica-circular-desde-agua-amarga-parque-natural-del-cabo-de-gata-nijar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Rellana Artesica — circular desde Agua Amarga&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.0 miles (21.0 km) long and winds through scenic canyons. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013569434/volcan-de-la-granitilla-circular-desde-nijar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Volcán de la Granitilla — circular desde Níjar&lt;/a&gt;, covering 12.5 miles (20.1 km).

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the trails?
A: Níjar's trails offer access to stunning natural monuments and historical sites. You can explore the dramatic volcanic landscapes, visit the historic mining areas like those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013424722/cortijo-del-fraile-and-rodalquilar-mines-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortijo del Fraile and Rodalquilar Mines Loop&lt;/a&gt;, or discover coastal gems like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/125186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Genoveses Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1065889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sirens' Reef&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2903173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Vela Blanca&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views.

Q: Is mountain biking allowed year-round in Níjar?
A: Yes, Níjar's consistent and pleasant climate makes it an accessible destination for mountain biking year-round. However, it's advisable to check local weather conditions, especially during the hotter summer months, and plan rides for cooler parts of the day.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Níjar offers 18 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders seeking technical descents and demanding climbs. These routes often traverse the more rugged volcanic terrain and provide significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Níjar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the diverse terrain ranging from arid canyons to coastal paths, and the opportunity to explore the natural beauty of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Níjar?
A: Yes, many routes in Níjar are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030977349/canon-de-rellana-artesica-circular-desde-agua-amarga-parque-natural-del-cabo-de-gata-nijar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Rellana Artesica — circular desde Agua Amarga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017939481/minas-del-oro-circular-desde-fernan-perez-parque-natural-del-cabo-de-gata-nijar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Minas del Oro — circular desde Fernán Pérez&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the average duration for mountain bike trails in Níjar?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013424722/cortijo-del-fraile-and-rodalquilar-mines-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortijo del Fraile and Rodalquilar Mines Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 4 minutes, while the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030977349/canon-de-rellana-artesica-circular-desde-agua-amarga-parque-natural-del-cabo-de-gata-nijar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Rellana Artesica&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park?
A: The Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park is a protected area. While general access for mountain biking on established trails is usually permitted, it's always recommended to check the official park regulations for any specific restrictions or permit requirements, especially for certain sensitive areas or during particular seasons. You can find more information about the park's regulations on its official website or by consulting local tourist information centers.

Q: Can I find cafes or accommodation near the mountain bike trails in Níjar?
A: Yes, charming towns and villages within the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, such as San José and Agua Amarga, offer various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These places are often cyclist-friendly and provide convenient stops before or after your rides.

Q: What are the elevation profiles like on Níjar's mountain bike trails?
A: Elevation profiles vary greatly depending on the trail. You can find routes with gentle inclines, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030977349/canon-de-rellana-artesica-circular-desde-agua-amarga-parque-natural-del-cabo-de-gata-nijar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Rellana Artesica&lt;/a&gt; with around 260 meters of ascent, or more challenging options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013424722/cortijo-del-fraile-and-rodalquilar-mines-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortijo del Fraile and Rodalquilar Mines Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 790 meters of elevation gain.
tips: Inaugurated in 1850, the Isabel II reservoir was a failure due to its scarcity of rainwater and the large amount of mud formed by the sediments. Its initial objective was to irrigate the crops in the fields of Níjar and its surroundings, but it is currently in a poor state of conservation, with part of the structure in ruins. Even so, the place offers impressive views of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park,
Its origin dates back to about 6.2 million years ago. The crater, located in the town of Níjar, has a conical shape with the cone half collapsed and eroded.
